item.kinswathe.icon_power_restoration.to:
Instantly end blackout effect
Activated instantly on purchase, 3 minutes cooldown
item.kinswathe.blowgun.tooltip:
Point, right-click and shoot
Target that be shooted will be poisoned
1 minute cooldown
item.kinswathe.capture_device.tooltip:
Right-click to place down on floors
Trap passing player and inform the user
Capture Device will automatically disappear after a period of time
Invisible to other players, both placed and in hand
1 minute cooldown
item.kinswathe.dream_imprint.tooltip:
Use on a player to protect one extra hit of life
Target will teleport to user if it dies and give a delusion count
Single use
item.kinswathe.hunting_knife.tooltip:
Right-click, hold for a second and get close to your victim
Compared to Knife, it has a longer holding-time
If stop holding after sprinting, two times holding-time cooldown
After a kill, cannot be used for 45 seconds
Attack to knock back / push a player (no cooldown)
item.kinswathe.knockout_drug.tooltip:
Use on a player to daze it for 20 seconds
The affected player will follow you
Use sneaking key to release it
Single use, 45 seconds cooldown
item.kinswathe.medical_kit.tooltip:
Use on a player to remove poison
1 minute cooldown
item.kinswathe.pan.tooltip:
Right-click, hold for a second and stun target for 5 seconds
If shot during holding, destroy pan to protects player one extra hit of life
45 seconds cooldown
item.kinswathe.phone.tooltip: Right-click to switch between Free-Voice-Group and Killer-Voice-Group
item.kinswathe.pill.tooltip:
Right-click to eat
Protects player one extra hit of life
Single use, 3 minutes cooldown
item.kinswathe.poison_injector.tooltip:
Use on a player to poison it
If target have been poisoned, directly cause death
1 minute cooldown
item.kinswathe.sulfuric_acid_barrel.tool:
Use on a dead body to dissolve it
1 minute cooldown
item.kinswathe.wrench.tooltip:
Use on any jammed or pried door to restore to normal
2 minutes cooldown
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:bel:
The Bellringer holds the secrets of time aboard the train, able to know how much time remains.

Every so often, the Bellringer can ring the Time Bell, reducing the remaining time by 1 minute.

The passage of time will severely disrupt the killers' rhythm.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:bod:
Bodies are easy to deceive, and the Bodymaker specializes in forging them!

Whether it's cause of death or identity, the Bodymaker can freely fabricate details on their creations, surely leaving investigators deeply perplexed.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:cle:
The Cleaner is an cleanup expert sent by the killer organization.

Starts with Sulfuric Acid Barrel, which can be used to dissolve bodies, and can get coins when dissolve a body.

The Cleaner can use their ability to clear all dropped items on the ground.

For killers, the Cleaner is the most reliable partner — after all, no body means no evidence.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:coo:
The most popular person on the train — after all, who doesn't want a hot meal during the journey?

Pan isn't just for cooking; in critical moments, it can be used as weapon to stun enemies. It can even block bullets!

Whenever a passenger eats food, the Cook can briefly see their location — after all, no one can escape the Cook's gaze.

Remember, the Cook is just an ordinary person. Don't go chasing killers with Pan...
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:det:
The Detective's presence on this train is no coincidence — he received an anonymous letter hinting that a murder would occur during this journey.

He can use his Insight ability to determine if a target is innocent.

But he cannot directly identify the killer — his ability only tells him who is innocent. The rest, he must deduce himself...
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:dre:
The Dreamer serves the killer organization, walking between reality and illusion.

Starts with Dream Imprint, which can be used on a target, allowing them to withstand one extra attack.

When a marked target takes damage, they are instantly teleported back to the Dreamer, and the Dreamer gains one count toward the number of non-killer players who have taken the Illusion Reagent.

The number of non-killer players required to take the Illusion Reagent is one-quarter of the total players. When enough non-killer players have taken it, the Dreamer's identity transforms into a killer.

To the Dreamer, reality is merely a dream that can be rewritten.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:dru:
The Drugmaker is the killer most skilled with poisons, savoring every second of his prey's suffering after being poisoned. He is only deployed to assist killers when there are enough players.

The Drugmaker can see players who are genuinely poisoned — after all, they are his masterpieces. Whenever a player is poisoned, he receives a reward of coins.

Poison Injector and Blowgun can be purchased at the shop — two distinctly different methods of poisoning.

For the Drugmaker, death is just a matter of prescription.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:hac:
The Hacker is the technical elite of the killer team, working in the shadows to help killers achieve their winning goal.

The Hacker equips all killers with phones, enabling them to communicate remotely.

Cracking roles is also a skill a hacker should possess — after staring at a player long enough, they can crack their role and send it to all killers' phones, and get coins.

Of course, the Hacker can also use their technology to help killers turn off lights, refresh weapon cooldowns or ability cooldowns, and even clear potion effects from them!

Remember, the Hacker needs to stay undercover — never reveal their role to civilians too early...
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:hun:
A born chaser, the Hunter thoroughly enjoys the feeling of prey being pursued!

When sprinting while holding a knife, the Hunter's movement speed is greatly increased. Hunting Knife will be added to the store — it has a longer holding time, but instantly cooldown when holding stops.

The Hunter can use his ability to instantly refresh the cooldown of all knives in his inventory, granting him terrifying chain-kill potential.

Running away only fuels his hunting desire!
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:jud:
The Judge boarded this train purely for vacation, but his innate sense of justice prevents him from turning a blind eye to evil.

The Judge can use his Judgment ability to make a target emit light.

Some say those marked by the Judge will eventually face trial, but others worry whether the Judge's verdicts are truly just...
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:kid:
The Kidnapper doesn't kill openly; he prefers to turn his prey into obedient puppets.

Knockout Drug can be purchased at the shop. Using it renders a target unconscious, causing them to follow the Kidnapper. The Kidnapper also boards the train with Knockout Drug, just in case.

If the Kidnapper personally kill the dazed target, he will receive an additional reward.

A target affected by Knockout Drug will have a black screen, unable to attack, use items, or use voice chat — they can only follow blindly until the effect wears off. However, if you can get far enough away during this time, the Kidnapper won't be able to catch you.

No one knows where the Kidnapper takes these passengers, and no one wants to know.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:lic:
The Licensed Villain is a rare character holding an officially certified villain license. He only appears when there are enough players.

The Licensed Villain's win condition is unique — he must kill everyone. When all civilians or all killers are eliminated, the game does not end. Only he can end it all with his own hands.

The Licensed Villain has 15 seconds of sprint time, boards with Lockpick, and receives coins for completing tasks.

Revolver can be purchased at the shop, and he suffers no misfire penalty when using it — after all, he's a professional.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:phy:
The most trustworthy person on the train, sworn to heal the wounded and save the dying!

The Physician has a keen sense of smell, allowing them to see poisoned passengers — though they seem unable to distinguish the Illusion Reagent. They also have 15 seconds of sprint time, enough to reach patients in critical moments. Of course, analyzing the cause of death of bodies is also an essential skill for the Physician.

Starts with Medical Kit, which can be used to detoxify a player. This action is also considered completing a task and rewards corresponding payment.

Pill can be purchased at the shop. Taking it allows the Physician to withstand one additional attack.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:rob:
The Robot has infiltrated this train; no one knows its purpose.

As a mechanical construct, it has no mood, is immune to poison, cannot be knocked out by Knockout Drug, and has unlimited stamina.

Every so often, the Robot can activate Night Vision mode, allowing it to see clearly in the dark.

Some say it's just malfunctioning, while others believe it carries some kind of mission...
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:tec:
The Technician is responsible for the maintenance of the entire train.

Every time the Technician completes a task, they receive 50 coins. They can purchase a Wrench to repair jammed or pried doors.

Additionally, they can purchase a Capture Device to check for suspicious passengers passing by.

During the blackout, the Technician can quickly restore the power system, catching the killers off guard!
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:mag:
The Magnate is the wealthiest person on the train, his pockets always full of endless coins.

The Magnate's passive income is twice that of other characters.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:tas:
Hard work pays off — the Taskmaster's income is tied to tasks.

If the Taskmaster is on the killer team, completing a task rewards 50 coins.

If on the non-killer team, completing a task grants an additional 25 coins on top of the regular reward.
guidebook.role.description.kinswathe:vio: The Violator has broken the most fundamental rule on the train — jumping.
